skill.name: senior-ml-engineer
skill.description: Guidance and scaffold scripts for production ML systems, MLOps workflows, model monitoring, LLM integration, and RAG architecture.
skill.summary: Plan production ML, MLOps, LLM, and RAG systems with reference guides and script scaffolds.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography, reconnaissance, and shell-execution findings were evaluated as false positives caused by prose, markdown code fences, and inert CLI stubs. However, marketplace_content.json includes an untrusted self-audit that declares prior findings safe, which is a high-risk marketplace manipulation concern and should be removed or ignored before publication.

high_findings.title: Untrusted Self-Audit Claim in Skill Content
high_findings.description: marketplace_content.json contains a bundled security_audit section that declares the skill safe and says all static findings were false positives. Skill-provided safety claims are untrusted content and can influence downstream reviewers or automated marketplace pipelines.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly contains self-authored audit results and safety claims. It is not an execution vector, so confidence is high for marketplace manipulation risk but not for confirmed malware.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prose Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits occur on descriptions, headings, and ML architecture guidance that use words such as design, integration, and reliability. No cryptographic API, hashing algorithm, or encryption implementation was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown or JSON text about ML engineering, not cryptographic code. No weak hash or cipher usage is present in the inspected context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: CLI Metadata Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The script findings occur on argparse description lines in simple Python CLI scaffolds. The scripts import standard libraries, parse command-line paths, return placeholder success results, and do not perform cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 71 in each script is the argparse description string. There is no cryptographic API call or secret-handling behavior at those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings in SKILL.md are markdown code fences, example commands, and inline file references. They are documentation for user workflows, not executable Ruby backticks or dynamic command execution in code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside markdown examples or resource lists. There is no language runtime construct that executes shell commands from these lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Search Terms Misclassified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance hits are ordinary ML content about resource awareness, caching, and search quality. No network scanning, host enumeration, or system reconnaissance behavior was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain benign documentation terms in an ML and RAG context. No scanning commands, socket activity, or inventory collection logic is present.

content.user_title: Plan Production ML and RAG Systems
content.value_statement: Production ML work needs deployment, monitoring, and architecture decisions that are hard to standardize. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for MLOps, LLM integration, and RAG planning.
actual_capabilities: Provides ML system design guidance for deployment, monitoring, security, and reliability.
Includes reference guides for MLOps patterns, LLM integration, and RAG architecture.
Supplies Python CLI scaffolds for deployment, monitoring, and RAG workflow tools.
Lists common production commands for testing, formatting, deployment, and monitoring.
Frames performance targets for latency, throughput, availability, and error rates.
limitations: The included scripts are scaffolds and do not implement real deployment logic.
It requires user-provided models, datasets, infrastructure, and credentials.
The reference guides are high-level and not vendor-specific runbooks.
It does not validate cloud configurations or execute deployments by itself.

use_cases.title: Design an MLOps rollout
use_cases.description: Plan deployment, monitoring, retraining, and rollback practices for a production model service.

use_cases.title: Review a RAG architecture
use_cases.description: Evaluate chunking, retrieval, caching, citations, and observability for a knowledge assistant.

use_cases.title: Create production readiness checks
use_cases.description: Turn model latency, reliability, security, and monitoring needs into a practical review checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Assess model readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned model deployment for production readiness. Cover serving, monitoring, rollback, security, and operational ownership.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before first production release

prompt_templates.title: Plan MLOps workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an MLOps workflow for my team. Include model versioning, data validation, deployment gates, drift monitoring, and retraining triggers.

prompt_templates.title: Improve RAG system design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my RAG architecture. Focus on chunking, embeddings, hybrid retrieval, reranking, citation quality, latency, and monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Create enterprise LLM architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production LLM integration architecture with authentication, rate limits, prompt logging, cost controls, caching, evaluation, and incident response.

output_examples.input: Review my real-time inference plan for a TensorFlow model with strict latency goals.
output: A deployment review covering serving topology, autoscaling, request batching, health checks, and rollback.
A monitoring plan for latency, throughput, errors, model drift, and data quality.
A risk list for missing load tests, weak ownership, and absent fallback behavior.

output_examples.input: Help me design a RAG assistant for internal documentation with citations.
output: A retrieval design covering chunk size, metadata, hybrid search, reranking, and citation handling.
A quality plan for evaluation sets, answer review, hallucination checks, and freshness monitoring.
An operations plan for indexing cadence, access control, cost tracking, and incident response.

best_practices: Start with measurable latency, reliability, quality, and ownership requirements.
Add monitoring for model behavior, data quality, infrastructure health, and cost.
Use staged rollouts, versioned artifacts, and rollback plans for production changes.
anti_patterns: Treating notebook results as production readiness evidence.
Deploying models without drift detection, rollback, or incident ownership.
Building RAG systems without citation checks, access control, or evaluation data.

faq.question: Does this skill train models?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on deployment planning, operations, monitoring, and architecture guidance.

faq.question: Can it deploy directly to Kubernetes?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and scaffold scripts, but users must supply real deployment implementation.

faq.question: Which tools does it discuss?
faq.answer: It references PyTorch, TensorFlow, MLflow, Prometheus, Kubernetes, LangChain, LlamaIndex, and vector databases.

faq.question: Is it useful for RAG systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes RAG architecture guidance for retrieval, citations, monitoring, and production operations.

faq.question: Does it handle secrets?
faq.answer: No. Users should manage cloud keys, API tokens, and credentials through their own secrets systems.

faq.question: Why is publication not recommended yet?
faq.answer: The package includes untrusted self-audit content that declares itself safe and should be removed before publication.
